spec: RFC-0008 §1.1, RFC 8949 §3 major type 5, §4.2.1 strict deterministic encoding incl. canonical-key-order
notes: Map keys must be strictly monotonically increasing in bytewise lexicographic order of their canonical CBOR encoding (RFC 8949 §4.2.1). Duplicate keys are forbidden; out-of-order keys are forbidden. The decoder enforces this against the input buffer; the encoder enforces it against the buffer of previously-emitted keys. Per the test-vectors README, output_hex is the authoritative byte sequence; byte_breakdown is a developer-readable annotation.

vectors.name: {1: "one", 2: "two"}
vectors.notes: header 0xa2 (map, 2 pairs); key1 uint 1; value1 text 'one' (header 0x63 + payload 'one'); key2 uint 2; value2 text 'two'
single-pair map; key uint 1 value uint 10

vectors.name: [{1:10}, {2:20}] (mixed array-of-maps)
vectors.notes: outer 0x82 array(2); first item map{1:10}; second item map{2:20}

vectors.name: {"a": 1, "b": 2}
vectors.notes: Text keys. key1 'a' is encoded as header 0x61 (major 3, length 1) + payload 0x61 ('a'). key2 'b' is encoded as 0x61 + 0x62. Lexicographic ordering on canonical encodings: prefix 0x61 matches, second byte 0x61 < 0x62, so 'a' < 'b'.

vectors.name: {"a": 1, "aa": 2} (length tiebreak: shorter key first)
vectors.notes: key1 'a' encodes as 0x61 0x61. key2 'aa' encodes as 0x62 (header major 3 length 2) + 0x61 0x61 (payload). Per §4.2.1: 0x61 < 0x62 in first byte of encoding, so 'a' < 'aa'.

negative_vectors.name: unsorted keys (key 2 before key 1)
negative_vectors.notes: Map header announces 2 pairs. First pair: key uint 2, value text 'two'. Second pair starts with key uint 1, which is less than 2. Decoder rejects on second key.

negative_vectors.name: duplicate key
negative_vectors.notes: Key uint 1 appears twice. Strict monotone order requires strict greater-than, so duplicates are rejected.

negative_vectors.name: indefinite-length map
negative_vectors.notes: initial byte 0xbf = major 5, info 31 (indefinite); forbidden by §4.2.1.

follow_up: These vector files will be re-emitted as canonical-CBOR TestVector per RFC-0008 §8 once the CBOR encoder is feature-complete (tags + bool + null implemented and ants_cbor_is_canonical functional).
